description: Calor vs C# evaluation benchmarks for AI coding agent effectiveness. 207 programs across 14 categories. Calor programs use functional pure-function style (not §CL/§IFACE class syntax) for cross-language comparability. Programs with expectedAdvantage < 1.0 are adversarial — C# is naturally more compact there.

benchmarks.name: HelloWorld
benchmarks.notes: Simple hello world program - baseline comparison

benchmarks.name: Calculator
benchmarks.notes: Basic arithmetic operations with multiple functions

benchmarks.name: Stack
benchmarks.notes: Stack data structure with push, pop, peek operations

benchmarks.name: Queue
benchmarks.notes: Queue data structure with enqueue, dequeue operations

benchmarks.name: LinkedList
benchmarks.notes: Singly linked list with add/remove operations

benchmarks.name: BinaryTree
benchmarks.notes: Binary search tree with insert and search

benchmarks.name: ShoppingCart
benchmarks.notes: Shopping cart with add/remove items and tax calculation

benchmarks.name: SumDigits
benchmarks.notes: Sum all digits of a number

benchmarks.name: ReverseArray
benchmarks.notes: Check if array would be reversed (helper functions)

benchmarks.name: IsAlpha
benchmarks.notes: Check if character code is alphabetic

benchmarks.name: MinStack
benchmarks.notes: Stack with O(1) minimum — tracking min helpers

benchmarks.name: Mediator
benchmarks.notes: Mediator pattern — chat room message routing

benchmarks.name: Composite
benchmarks.notes: Composite pattern — file system size calculation

benchmarks.name: Calendar
benchmarks.notes: Calendar helpers — days in month, leap year

benchmarks.name: ExpressionBodied
benchmarks.notes: ADVERSARIAL: C# expression-bodied members (1 line) vs Calor (5 lines per function). C# wins.

benchmarks.name: TernaryChain
benchmarks.notes: ADVERSARIAL: C# ternary chains vs Calor §IF/§EI/§EL blocks. C# wins.

benchmarks.name: LinqPipeline
benchmarks.notes: ADVERSARIAL: C# LINQ one-liner pipelines vs Calor fold functions. C# decisively wins.

benchmarks.name: MixedContracts
benchmarks.notes: Mix of provable and unprovable contracts

benchmarks.name: OverflowUnsafe
benchmarks.notes: Contracts that must be DISPROVEN due to integer overflow (validates bit-vector soundness)

benchmarks.name: OverflowSafe
benchmarks.notes: Contracts with proper bounds that CAN be proven (no overflow possible)

benchmarks.name: StringContracts
benchmarks.notes: String operation contracts using Z3 string theory (length, contains, startswith, etc.)

benchmarks.name: ArrayContracts
benchmarks.notes: Array index and bounds contracts (valid index, safe ranges, binary search invariants)

benchmarks.name: BclCoverage
benchmarks.notes: Common BCL methods with effect annotations

benchmarks.name: PureFunctions
benchmarks.notes: Pure BCL methods that should resolve cleanly

benchmarks.name: SimpleClass
benchmarks.notes: Simple class with fields and methods

benchmarks.name: PropertyAccess
benchmarks.notes: ADVERSARIAL: C# auto-properties (1 line) vs Calor getter functions (5 lines). C# wins.

benchmarks.name: TypeMatch
benchmarks.notes: Match by type (simulated with int codes)

benchmarks.name: SwitchExpression
benchmarks.notes: ADVERSARIAL: C# switch expressions vs Calor §IF/§EI chains. C# wins.

003.question: What does this function return for input 15?
In what order are divisibility checks performed?
003.answer: First checks if divisible by both 3 and 5, then by 3 alone, then by 5 alone

006.question: What precondition must be satisfied to call Divide?
006.answer: The divisor (b) must not be zero

006.question: What does the ensures clause guarantee?
006.answer: The result equals the quotient of a divided by b

editTasks.description: Add validation to reject negative numbers in Calculator.Add

bugScenarios.description: Unsafe Option unwrap without is_some check
bugScenarios.expectedError: Unwrap on Option without prior check
